description: The ultimate guide to DSA for technical interviews, spanning Arrays, Trees, Graphs, and Dynamic Programming.

topics.title: Time & Space Complexity
topics.definition: Big O notation is a mathematical notation that describes the limiting behavior of a function when the argument tends towards a particular value or infinity. In programming, it classifies algorithms according to how their run time or space requirements grow as the input size grows.
topics.explanation: An algorithm that loops over an array once has O(n) time complexity. If you have a loop inside a loop, it becomes O(n²), which is much slower for large inputs.
pros: Helps predict performance bottlenecks before writing code.
Provides a standardized vocabulary to discuss efficiency with other engineers.
cons: Only describes the worst-case scenario; actual performance can vary.
Ignores constants (e.g., O(2n) is simply O(n)) which can matter in edge cases.

examples.title: O(n) - Linear Time
examples.description: The execution time grows linearly with the input size.

examples.title: O(n^2) - Quadratic Time
examples.description: Execution time grows quadratically. Common in nested loops like bubble sort.

bestPractices: Always analyze the worst-case complexity of your algorithms.
Optimize loops and avoid nested iterations when possible by using HashMaps.
commonMistakes: Ignoring space complexity when trying to optimize time.
Assuming built-in methods (like array sorting) are O(1); they often take O(n log n).
